req.title: Recurring transfer (negative variant)
req.statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-055 defines two equivalence classes for per-occurrence limits (premium ≤ $10,000; standard ≤ $2,500), explicit 422 error paths for limit-exceeded and unsupported-frequency, and boundary values at exactly $2,500 (standard cap) and $10,000 (premium cap). The suite covers: (1) EP positive – premium customer schedules a valid recurring transfer well within limit; (2) NEG – standard customer exceeds the $2,500 cap, expects 422; (3) BVA – standard customer schedules transfer at exactly $2,500 (on-boundary, must succeed); (4) NEG – unsupported frequency yields 422; (5) DT – premium customer at exactly $10,000 (premium boundary, must succeed) cross-referenced against tier×amount decision table. All tests use a shared fixture for auth tokens and a helper for POST calls, keeping each test under 40 lines.

tests.name: test_premium_customer_valid_recurring_transfer
trace: R-FIN-055: premium tier may schedule up to $10,000 per occurrence
R-FIN-055: schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_premium_customer_valid_recurring_transfer(api_client, premium_auth_token):
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "source_account": "<premium_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body, "Missing schedule_id in response"
    assert "next_run" in body, "Missing next_run in response"
    assert body["amount"] == 5000

tests.name: test_standard_customer_exceeds_2500_limit_returns_422
expected: Response body error code indicates per-occurrence limit exceeded
trace: R-FIN-055: non-premium accounts capped at $2,500 per occurrence
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_standard_customer_exceeds_2500_limit_returns_422(api_client, standard_auth_token):
    payload = {
        "amount": 2501,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "source_account": "<standard_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {standard_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "Missing error detail in 422 response"
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "limit" in error_text or "exceed" in error_text, "Error message should reference limit"

tests.name: test_standard_customer_at_exact_2500_boundary_succeeds
trace: R-FIN-055: non-premium cap is $2,500; on-boundary value must be accepted
R-FIN-055: schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_standard_customer_at_exact_2500_boundary_succeeds(api_client, standard_auth_token):
    payload = {
        "amount": 2500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "source_account": "<standard_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {standard_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at boundary $2500, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body, "Missing schedule_id"
    assert "next_run" in body, "Missing next_run"
    assert body["amount"] == 2500

tests.name: test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422
expected: Response body error references unsupported frequency
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422(api_client, premium_auth_token):
    payload = {
        "amount": 1000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "HOURLY",
        "source_account": "<premium_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for unsupported frequency, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "frequency" in error_text or "unsupported" in error_text, "Error should mention frequency"

import pytest

def test_premium_customer_at_exact_10000_boundary_succeeds(api_client, premium_auth_token):
    payload = {
        "amount": 10000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",

tests.name: test_tier_amount_decision_table_premium_exceeds_10000_returns_422
expected: Response body error indicates per-occurrence limit exceeded for premium tier
trace: R-FIN-055: premium tier cap is $10,000; $10,001 must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_tier_amount_decision_table_premium_exceeds_10000_returns_422(api_client, premium_auth_token):
    payload = {
        "amount": 10001,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "source_account": "<premium_account_id>",
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for premium amount>10000, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "limit" in error_text or "exceed" in error_text, "Error should reference limit breach"
